summary: Stripe-removal + manual-payment confirmation flow fully verified, backend + frontend. 16/16 backend pytest cases pass (Stripe routes now 404, site-settings exposes 14 payment fields, PUT persists them, public confirm-payment happy/422/400-paid/400-void all correct, mark-paid sets paid_at + idempotent + 404, PDF renders HOW TO PAY section with bank+PayPal details, list/CRUD/send pre-existing endpoints intact). Frontend Playwright verifies all 4 method cards render, bank toggle expands and shows IBAN, validation toast on empty reference, success toast + acknowledgement block on submit, admin SiteSettings persists IBAN/PayPal.Me/Revtag/contact email across reload, confirmation badge appears on row, Mark-as-Paid button flips status to PAID and disappears, confirmation badge removed after mark-paid.

issues: Payment groups render with `border border-mir-border p-6 bg-white` only — no visual separator between the Logo section and the 4 payment cards. Minor; consider a faint heading above the payment block (currently it's just a paragraph).

action_items: Delete /app/backend/tests/test_iteration8_regression.py OR rewrite it — it still contains the Stripe-checkout test (asserts /api/invoices/public/{token}/checkout returns 200) which will now fail because the endpoint is intentionally removed. The review-request note claimed it was rewritten; it was not. There is also a syntax-stub-duplication artifact at lines 417-422 (`h failed: ...` etc) that breaks importability.
Optional: extract /api/site-settings into a shared React context — Navbar + Footer + PublicInvoice + admin panel each call it independently (carry-over from iter_10).
critical_code_review_comments: /app/backend/tests/test_iteration8_regression.py is BROKEN at line 417 (`h failed: {r2.status_code}` is dangling outside any function — Python syntax-error on import) AND the TestInvoicesFullFlow.test_create_public_pdf_payment_flow still asserts the now-removed Stripe checkout endpoint. Either delete the file or rewrite to use /confirm-payment + /mark-paid.

admin_invoices.py:_payment_settings strips logo_url but does NOT strip other non-payment fields that might exist in site_settings (e.g., future branding fields). Safer to keep an allow-list of payment_* / bank_* / paypal_* / revolut_* keys.
PaymentConfirmationPayload accepts unlimited 'note' length up to 2000 chars but allows empty string. Backend currently coerces empty string to None — that is intentional and tested.

InvoicesPanel uses native `window.confirm()` for Mark-as-Paid (per dialog auto-accept needed in the playwright run). Native confirms are not styleable and can be blocked by some browsers. Consider a shadcn AlertDialog for consistency with other destructive actions.

/app/frontend/src/pages/PublicInvoice.jsx: 'no method selected' produces a console-level inline error string (`Please choose a payment method first.`) but the playwright run showed NO sonner toast on that path (toast empty). The empty-reference toast works. Verify: when user clicks Submit without picking a method, the toast.error() call should fire — it may be that the form `<button disabled={...}>` is suppressing the click before the handler runs. Re-check the disable logic.
seed_data_creation: Created TEST_Frontend Client invoice (INV-2026-0010, token=7xFIswPdftbCiqQ6zXmcEA) — submitted bank confirmation TXN-9999 then marked paid via the admin UI. Site settings updated to working bank/PayPal/Revolut/contact values during the run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Preview URL: https://mir-consulting-next.preview.emergentagent.com. Pytest file: /app/backend/tests/test_iteration11_payment_flow.py (16 tests, ~8s runtime). Frontend selectors confirmed working: pay-method-{bank|paypal|revolut|contact}, pay-method-{m}-toggle, payment-confirmation-{submit|reference|note|acknowledged}, payment-group-{m}, payment-field-{key}, site-settings-save, admin-invoice-confirmation-{id}, admin-invoice-mark-paid-{id}, admin-invoice-row-{id}. Mark-as-Paid uses native window.confirm — Playwright must `page.on('dialog', d => d.accept())`. ⚠️ /app/backend/tests/test_iteration8_regression.py is BROKEN and tests dead Stripe endpoint — DO NOT include it in regression run until fixed by main agent.

backend_pytest.TestConfirmPayment: 4/4 PASS (happy, 422 bad method, 400 when paid, 400 when void)
backend_pytest.TestPdfHowToPay: 1/1 PASS (PDF contains 'HOW TO PAY' + IBAN + PayPal extracted via pypdf)
backend_pytest.TestPreExisting: 3/3 PASS (list, send 503 with 'Email is not configured', public token GET)

frontend_public_invoice.empty_reference_validation_toast: Please enter a transaction reference or confirmation code.

frontend_admin_site_settings.all_4_payment_groups_render: yes (bank group queried once raced but its inner field DID render and persisted)

detailed_results.smtp_status: 503 'Email is not configured' on this preview pod — expected per review note, NOT a regression.

rca_of_the_issue: N/A — no defects in the new payment flow. The single point of friction is in the LEGACY test file test_iteration8_regression.py which still asserts the removed Stripe checkout endpoint and has a corrupt tail (line 417). That file was supposed to be rewritten according to the review request but wasn't; flagged for main agent to delete or rewrite.
